payload.text: Create a base for an AI PWA that allows users to upload worksheets of mathematics questions, create a dashbaord with their general progress like the blue one here but have original take and make it only black and white and minimal, create a part to showcase the question answering component and then create a marking demonstration use fake problems na donly example input etc

**Envisioning the PWA**

I'm focusing on the foundational elements of the AI-powered PWA. My priority is crafting a robust base specifically designed for math worksheets. I'm imagining a black and white interface, a stark contrast to the existing "blue" dashboard. This new visual design is a central point of focus, and I need to come up with some ideas for the aesthetic and feel of the interface.


**Refining the Interface**

I'm now zeroing in on the UI/UX details, aiming for a monochrome, minimalist aesthetic, inspired by that Leibniz image. I've decided to go with React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and the Gemini API. My architecture is taking shape with key components defined. The black-and-white theme will extend to dark/light mode, using Zinc/Slate/Neutral shades. I'm leaning toward a clean sans-serif font and simplified math rendering for now.

payload.text: change the name to PWA make it only white background and make it shittier, add input for stylus

**Refining the Project's Scope**

I'm focusing on the request to rename "Leibniz AI" to "PWA" and drastically simplify the design. The goal is now a minimal, white-background interface. The direction is to create a deliberately basic application. This will serve as a simplified starting point to iterate upon.


**Updating the Visual Style**

I'm now incorporating a "brutalist" aesthetic with sharp borders, and high contrast. I'm focusing on the canvas component for stylus input, aiming to capture the image and send it to the Gemini API with a "Capture & Solve" button, adding a drawing canvas next to the text input. The slate theme will need replacing by a gray theme.

payload.text: no make the titles be sensible and have a go button

**Refining Project Titles**

I'm focused on improving the app titles. The current naming is too obscure and not user-friendly. I'm moving away from jargon like "DATA_DUMP" and aiming for more intuitive, descriptive titles that will resonate with users and clearly indicate the function of each module. Thinking about titles that offer hints, or give useful information.

payload.text: normal font for titles and no underscores

**Refining App Aesthetics**

Okay, I'm making progress on the visual changes. I'm focusing on the typography first; it looks like we're moving away from the bold, uppercase, mono-spaced style for titles. Replacing this with a normal, clean font should make the interface more user-friendly. Also, no more underscores! I've flagged that for removal across all labels.


**Revising Text Style**

I'm adjusting the text styling to be more readable while maintaining the "shitty" aesthetic. The current plan involves using a standard sans-serif font for titles, like Inter, and keeping the rest in Courier Prime or a similar monospace font. I've updated the `index.html` file to reflect this, and I'm methodically updating labels in the sidebar and other components to Title Case. I'm also revising abbreviations to full phrases for clarity.

payload.text: Okay you need to add the problem yourself like an integration problem with someones example handwriting text and then allow me to press a button called mark which then creates boxes around working out to give feedback like red and green highlighted boxes

**Developing the Marking System**

I'm currently focused on the "Marking System" feature. I've decided to incorporate a default integration problem, complete with an example of handwritten content. My immediate task is adding a "Mark" button for enhanced marking functionality.


**Implementing Visual Feedback**

I'm now integrating visual cues, starting with rectangular boxes for feedback. These boxes will be overlaid on the "worksheet," changing color to indicate correctness (green) or error (red). I'm focusing on updating the `MarkingSystem.tsx` component and setting up a mock handwritten example using a styled `div` or simulated canvas. I'm simulating AI analysis to generate box coordinates for marking the handwritten content. I'm exploring the `gemini-3-pro-preview` model, or mocking the integration to define areas as the user desires.


**Refining the Marking System**

I'm now refining the `MarkingSystem` component. I'm adding `isMarked` and `feedbackBoxes` states. I've got a container for the simulated "Handwritten Worksheet". The "GO" button is being renamed to "MARK" as requested. I'm updating `geminiService.ts` to support bounding boxes, or I'll mock the coordinates in the component, since I need to simulate or structure an output.

entries.description: Update Marking System to include a simulated handwritten worksheet, a 'MARK' button, and overlay feedback boxes (red/green) on top of the working out.

payload.text:
change worksheet solver to problems and then give a problem like integral that accepts my handwriting input, recognises the handwriting input and then does the marking by converting handwriting to text for ai marking


change name from PWA and pwa system root to TL, get rid of ai online and alpha build,

**Refining Application Name**

I'm currently focused on renaming the application. I'm replacing all instances of "PWA" and "PWA System Root" with "TL". The goal is to ensure consistency across all elements of the application. I'll need to double check the user interface to confirm all instances have been updated to ensure the user has the best experience.


**Updating Tab Titles**

I've renamed "Worksheet Solver" to "Problems" across the tab and content. Specifically, I'm working on the handwriting input integration. The focus is to allow users to input a problem, convert it to text, and then use AI for marking. I'm updating `Sidebar.tsx` to reflect these changes.


**Implementing Handwriting Recognition**

I'm working on integrating handwriting recognition into the "Problems" component. My current focus is converting the canvas drawing to a base64 string, which will then be sent to Gemini. I'm crafting a prompt that instructs the AI to both transcribe the math from the image and evaluate the solution steps, providing the recognized text and feedback to the user. I've realized I need to refine the prompt further to address both transcription and grading in a combined approach.


**Refining AI Integration**

I'm now focused on using the Gemini API effectively. I'm removing the "AI Online" and "Alpha Build" indicators in the header, replacing them with "TL" for simplicity. I'm updating `index.html` with the title "TL" and modifying the `App.tsx` header accordingly. In the `Sidebar.tsx`, I'm changing "PWA" to "TL" and "Solve" to "Problems". The core update is in `Problems.tsx`, where I'm hardcoding a sample problem for initial testing and refining the image-to-Gemini prompt to include both transcription and grading instructions, returning the recognized text, score, and feedback.


**Clarifying User Request**

I'm now revisiting the "red and green boxes" feedback request. It seems the user wants this functionality, but I need to clarify if it's for the "Problems" tab (formerly "Worksheet Solver") or just a future "Marking" demo. The original request involved handwriting input, problem solving, and marking. Given the challenges of dynamically creating bounding boxes from handwriting images, and considering Gemini 1.5/2.0 might be able to generate bounding box information, I've decided to prioritize OCR and text-based marking for now in "Problems", and keep the visual box demo separate for later. I'll focus on the recognition and text-based marking for the "Problems" tab.
